assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Alexey, I can see the distributed team is growing and Wise + crypto is no longer scaling without manual overhead. Mellow gives you a structured payout flow: automatic payments, statuses without follow-ups, escalations handled by us — not by you. What breaks most often in the current setup?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.
criteria.evidence_quote: The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically.
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

criteria.name: Language fit
criteria.short_reason: Tone and delivery are not adapted to the chosen persona.
criteria.evidence_quote: Alexey, I can see the distributed team is growing and Wise + crypto is no longer scaling without manual overhead. Mellow gives you a structured payout flow: automatic payments, statuses without follow-ups, escalations handled by us — not by you. What breaks most often in the current setup?
criteria.label: Persona language fit

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Alexey, I can see the distributed team is growing and Wise + crypto is no longer scaling without manual overhead. Mellow gives you a structured payout flow: automatic payments, statuses without follow-ups, escalations handled by us — not by you. What breaks most often in the current setup?

objections.objection: Ok but crypto + Wise has worked for two years. Why do I need this specifically now?
Fine. Send a brief: what disappears from manual, case study, rough numbers.
Waiting for the materials. When will you send them?
Send it. If it's concrete — I'll respond.

criteria.label: Core objection handling

criteria.name: Next step

Mellow gives you a structured payout flow: automatic payments, statuses without follow-ups, escalations handled by us — not by you. What breaks most often in the current setup?
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Alexey, I can see the distributed team is growing and Wise + crypto is no longer scaling without manual overhead. Mellow gives you a structured payout flow: automatic payments, statuses without follow-ups, escalations handled by us — not by you. What breaks most often in the current setup?
Ok but crypto + Wise has worked for two years. Why do I need this specifically now?

The current setup stops being defensible in two scenarios: a payment gets stuck with no SLA path — or diligence comes and there's no audit trail. Your current setup has both risks. Mellow removes them specifically.
Fine. Send a brief: what disappears from manual, case study, rough numbers.

Specifically what stops being manual: payment status follow-ups, exception handling, and reconciliation. Mellow owns the payment workflow, KYC checks, contractor documents, and incident handling on our side, while auto-payments and real-time statuses replace the chat threads and spreadsheet updates. What would take the most pain off your team specifically?
Waiting for the materials. When will you send them?

Specifically what stops being manual: payment status follow-ups, exception handling, and reconciliation. Mellow owns the payment workflow, KYC checks, contractor documents, and incident handling on our side, while auto-payments and real-time statuses replace the chat threads and spreadsheet updates. Which part of your process is eating the most time right now?
Send it. If it's concrete — I'll respond.
